description: Dependency versions used by CI come from the active lockfile and package manager.
body: Use when changing dependencies or diagnosing version drift. Do not update package.json without the matching lockfile when the repository tracks one. Use the manager indicated by lockfile/packageManager. Verify with install or tests under that manager. Risk boundary is accidental broad dependency upgrades. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
